title: Word Search II
description:
You are given `board`, a 2-D grid of single-character strings, and a list `words`. A word is found on the board if it can be traced by a path that starts at any cell and moves to horizontally or vertically adjacent cells, using each grid cell at most once per path. Return the list of all words from `words` that can be found on the board (each qualifying word appears once, in any order).

**Example**

```
board = [
  ["o","a","a","n"],
  ["e","t","a","e"],
  ["i","h","k","r"],
  ["i","f","l","v"]
]
words = ["oath","pea","eat","rain"]  ->  ["oath","eat"]
```
starter_code:
def find_words(board, words):
    # board: list of rows of single-character strings. words: list[str].
    # Return the list of words from `words` that can be traced on the board.
    pass
hints: Insert all target words into a trie so shared prefixes are explored together instead of re-scanning the board once per word.

DFS from every board cell walking the trie in step; when a trie node marks the end of a word, record it and delete that marker so it can't be re-added, and prune trie branches (and skip re-descending them) once they have no children left to find.

solutions.name: Trie + DFS backtracking with pruning
solutions.explanation: All words are inserted into a shared trie so that DFS explores the board and the trie in lock-step, sharing work across words with common prefixes. Starting DFS from every board cell, at each step we only continue into a neighbor if the trie has an edge for that neighbor's letter. Whenever a trie node marks the end of a word, that word is recorded in the results and the end-marker is deleted so it can never be added twice (multiple paths could otherwise reach the same word). Trie nodes with no children left after a marker is removed are deleted from their parent, pruning the search space as words are found.
solutions.complexity: Time O(rows * cols * 4^L) where L is the longest word length, Space O(sum of word lengths) for the trie
